# azure-functions-scaffold — Full LLM Reference

> Scaffolding CLI for production-ready Azure Functions Python v2 projects.

## Package Info

- PyPI: `pip install azure-functions-scaffold`
- Version: 0.5.0
- Python: >=3.10, <3.15
- License: MIT
- Docs: https://yeongseon.github.io/azure-functions-scaffold-python/
- Repository: https://github.com/yeongseon/azure-functions-scaffold-python
- Azure Functions programming model: v2

## Installation

```bash
pip install azure-functions-scaffold
```

## Entry Points

- CLI: `azure-functions-scaffold` or `afs` (short alias)
- Python import: `from azure_functions_scaffold.scaffolder import scaffold_project`

## Complete CLI Reference

### Root Command

```bash
azure-functions-scaffold [GROUP] [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]
afs [GROUP] [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]
```

Root options:
- `--version` — Show installed version and exit
- `-h`, `--help` — Show help

Top-level utility commands:
- `afs new <project_name>` — **Shortcut** to create an API project (delegates to `afs api new`)
- `afs templates` — List available scaffold templates
- `afs presets` — List available project presets

#### `afs new <project_name>` (top-level shortcut)

Create an HTTP API project with production defaults. This is a shortcut for `afs api new`.

```bash
afs new <project_name> [OPTIONS]
```

Arguments:
- `project_name` (required) — Directory name for the new project

Options: Same as `afs api new` (see below).

### Subcommand Group: `api`

Purpose: intent-based API scaffolding and HTTP expansion.

#### `afs api new <project_name>`

Create an HTTP API project with opinionated defaults:
- Template: `http`
- Preset: `strict`
- Features: OpenAPI + validation + doctor

```bash
afs api new <project_name> [OPTIONS]
```

Arguments:
- `project_name` (required) — Directory name for the new project

Options:
- `-d, --destination PATH` (default: `.`)
- `--python-version VERSION` (default: `3.10`; choices: `3.10`, `3.11`, `3.12`, `3.13`, `3.14 (Preview)`)
- `--github-actions/--no-github-actions` (default: `--no-github-actions`)
- `--git/--no-git` (default: `--no-git`)
- `--azd/--no-azd` (default: `--no-azd`)
- `--dry-run` (default: `False`)
- `--overwrite` (default: `False`)

#### `afs api add <function_name>`

Add an HTTP function module to an existing scaffolded project.

```bash
afs api add <function_name> [OPTIONS]
```

Arguments:
- `function_name` (required) — Function name to add

Options:
- `--project-root PATH` (default: `.`)
- `--dry-run` (default: `False`)

#### `afs api add-route <route_name>`

Add a simple HTTP route (blueprint + test) to an existing scaffolded project.

```bash
afs api add-route <route_name> [OPTIONS]
```

Arguments:
- `route_name` (required) — Route name to add

Options:
- `--project-root PATH` (default: `.`)
- `--dry-run` (default: `False`)

#### `afs api add-resource <resource_name>`

Add a full CRUD resource (blueprint, service, schema, test) to an existing scaffolded project.

```bash
afs api add-resource <resource_name> [OPTIONS]
```

Arguments:
- `resource_name` (required) — Resource name to add (e.g. products)

Options:
- `--project-root PATH` (default: `.`)
- `--dry-run` (default: `False`)
### Subcommand Group: `worker`

Purpose: intent-based worker scaffolding for non-HTTP triggers.

Each command accepts this shape:

```bash
afs worker <trigger> <project_name> [OPTIONS]
```

Shared options for all worker commands:
- `-d, --destination PATH` (default: `.`)
- `--python-version VERSION` (default: `3.10`; choices: `3.10`, `3.11`, `3.12`, `3.13`, `3.14 (Preview)`)
- `--github-actions/--no-github-actions` (default: `--no-github-actions`)
- `--git/--no-git` (default: `--no-git`)
- `--azd/--no-azd` (default: `--no-azd`)
- `--dry-run` (default: `False`)
- `--overwrite` (default: `False`)

Worker commands:
- `afs worker timer <project_name>` — timer trigger, preset `standard`
- `afs worker queue <project_name>` — queue trigger, preset `standard`
- `afs worker blob <project_name>` — blob trigger, preset `standard`
- `afs worker servicebus <project_name>` — service bus trigger, preset `standard`
- `afs worker eventhub <project_name>` — event hub trigger, preset `standard`

### Subcommand Group: `ai`

Purpose: intent-based AI/agent project scaffolding.

#### `afs ai agent <project_name>`

Create a LangGraph agent scaffold:
- Template: `langgraph`
- Preset: `standard`

```bash
afs ai agent <project_name> [OPTIONS]
```

Arguments:
- `project_name` (required) — Directory name for the new project

Options:
- `-d, --destination PATH` (default: `.`)
- `--python-version VERSION` (default: `3.10`; choices: `3.10`, `3.11`, `3.12`, `3.13`, `3.14 (Preview)`)
- `--github-actions/--no-github-actions` (default: `--no-github-actions`)
- `--git/--no-git` (default: `--no-git`)
- `--azd/--no-azd` (default: `--no-azd`)
- `--dry-run` (default: `False`)
- `--overwrite` (default: `False`)

### Subcommand Group: `advanced`

Purpose: power-user mode with direct template/preset/feature control.

#### `afs advanced new <project_name>`

Create a project with explicit flags instead of intent defaults.

```bash
afs advanced new <project_name> [OPTIONS]
```

Arguments:
- `project_name` (required) — Directory name for the new project

Options (full set):
- `-d, --destination PATH` (default: `.`)
- `-t, --template TEXT` (default: `http`; choices: `http`, `timer`, `queue`, `blob`, `servicebus`, `eventhub`, `cosmosdb`, `durable`, `ai`, `langgraph`)
- `--preset TEXT` (default: `standard`; choices: `minimal`, `standard`, `strict`)
- `--python-version VERSION` (default: `3.10`; choices: `3.10`, `3.11`, `3.12`, `3.13`, `3.14 (Preview)`)
- `--github-actions/--no-github-actions` (default: `--no-github-actions`)
- `--git/--no-git` (default: `--no-git`)
- `--with-openapi/--no-openapi` (default: `--no-openapi`)
- `--with-validation/--no-validation` (default: `--no-validation`)
- `--with-doctor/--no-doctor` (default: `--no-doctor`)
- `--with-db/--no-db` (default: `--no-db`) *(planned — not yet available in CLI)*
- `--azd/--no-azd` (default: `--no-azd`)
- `--dry-run` (default: `False`)
- `--overwrite` (default: `False`)

#### `afs advanced add <trigger> <function_name>`

Add a function module to an existing scaffolded project for any supported trigger.

```bash
afs advanced add <trigger> <function_name> [OPTIONS]
```

Arguments:
- `trigger` (required) — Trigger type
- `function_name` (required) — Function name to add

Options:
- `--project-root PATH` (default: `.`)
- `--dry-run` (default: `False`)

Supported trigger values for `advanced add`:
- `http`, `timer`, `queue`, `blob`, `servicebus`, `eventhub`, `cosmosdb`, `durable`, `ai`, `langgraph`

#### `afs advanced templates`

Alias command under advanced group to list templates.

```bash
afs advanced templates
```

#### `afs advanced presets`

Alias command under advanced group to list presets.

```bash
afs advanced presets
```

#### `afs advanced add-route <route_name>`

Add a simple HTTP route to an existing scaffolded project (advanced group).

```bash
afs advanced add-route <route_name> [OPTIONS]
```

Arguments:
- `route_name` (required) — Route name to add

Options:
- `--project-root PATH` (default: `.`)
- `--dry-run` (default: `False`)

#### `afs advanced add-resource <resource_name>`

Add a full CRUD resource (blueprint, service, schema, test) to an existing scaffolded project (advanced group).

```bash
afs advanced add-resource <resource_name> [OPTIONS]
```

Arguments:
- `resource_name` (required) — Resource name to add (e.g. products)

Options:
- `--project-root PATH` (default: `.`)
- `--dry-run` (default: `False`)

## Common Workflows

### Workflow 1: API project with strict defaults

Python 3.14 is Preview on Azure Functions; default to 3.12 unless Preview support is verified.

```bash
afs new my-api
cd my-api
pip install -e .
func start
```

The top-level `afs new` is the fastest way to start. Equivalent to `afs api new`.

### Workflow 2: Worker project by trigger intent
### Workflow 3: Worker project by trigger intent

```bash
afs worker timer my-scheduler
afs worker queue my-queue-worker
afs worker eventhub my-stream-worker
```

Use trigger-specific worker intents instead of manually selecting templates.

### Workflow 4: Expand an existing project

```bash
cd my-api
# Add a simple route
afs api add-route status
# Add a full CRUD resource
afs api add-resource products
# Add HTTP function
afs api add get_orders
# Add non-HTTP triggers
afs advanced add queue sync_jobs
afs advanced add servicebus process_events
```

Use `api add-route` for simple endpoints, `api add-resource` for full CRUD, and `advanced add` for non-HTTP triggers.

### Workflow 5: Dry run preview

```bash
afs new my-api --dry-run
afs advanced new my-custom --template durable --dry-run
afs api add-resource products --project-root ./my-api --dry-run
afs advanced add blob ingest_reports --project-root ./my-api --dry-run
```

## Project Structure Generated

### HTTP/API scaffold

```text
my-api/
|- function_app.py
|- host.json
|- local.settings.json.example
|- pyproject.toml
|- app/
|  |- __init__.py
|  |- core/
|  |  |- __init__.py
|  |  |- config.py
|  |  `- logging.py
|  |- dependencies/
|  |  `- __init__.py
|  |- functions/
|  |  |- __init__.py
|  |  |- health.py
|  |  `- webhooks.py
|  |- schemas/
|  |  |- __init__.py
|  |  `- webhooks.py
|  `- services/
|     |- __init__.py
|     |- health_service.py
|     `- webhook_service.py
`- tests/
   |- __init__.py
   |- test_health.py
   `- test_webhooks.py
```

### Worker scaffolds

Similar structure with trigger-specific function modules (timer, queue, blob, servicebus, eventhub).

## Environment

### Generated `local.settings.json.example`

```json
{
  "IsEncrypted": false,
  "Values": {
    "AzureWebJobsStorage": "UseDevelopmentStorage=true",
    "FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME": "python",
    "AzureWebJobsFeatureFlags": "EnableWorkerIndexing"
  },
  "Host": {
    "CORS": {
      "AllowedOrigins": ["*"]
    }
  }
}
```

Copy to `local.settings.json` and adjust connection values for local development.

## Presets

- `minimal` — Bare essentials only
- `standard` — Ruff + pytest
- `strict` — Ruff + mypy + pytest

## Error Handling

| Error | Cause | Solution |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Project name must start with alphanumeric character` | Invalid project name format | Use letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores only |
| `Unknown template '{name}'` | Invalid template choice | Use `afs templates` to inspect valid template names |
| `Unknown preset '{name}'` | Invalid preset choice | Use `afs presets` to inspect valid preset names |
| `Unsupported Python version '{version}'` | Invalid Python version | Use: `3.10`, `3.11`, `3.12`, `3.13`, `3.14 (Preview)` |
| `Target directory already exists` | Destination path exists | Use `--overwrite` or a different destination |
| `Project root does not look like a scaffolded Azure Functions project.` | Invalid project path for add commands | Ensure `function_app.py` and `app/functions` exist |
| `Unsupported trigger '{name}'` | Invalid trigger in `advanced add` | Use supported triggers from CLI help |
| `Function module already exists: ...` | Duplicate function name | Use another function name or remove existing module |

## Tips for LLM Integration

- This package is a CLI-first scaffold generator
- Use `afs new my-api` as the fastest way to create a project
- Prefer intent commands (`api`, `worker`, `ai`) for default best-practice setups
- Use `advanced` commands only when direct template/feature control is required
- Use `--dry-run` to inspect planned file operations before writing
- `afs new my-api` (or `afs api new my-api`) already includes strict API defaults; do not add manual strict/OpenAPI/validation flags unless you intentionally switch to `afs advanced new`
- Use `afs api add-route` for lightweight endpoints and `afs api add-resource` for full CRUD expansion
- Generated projects keep business logic in `app/services` and trigger bindings in `app/functions`
- Project configuration is centered in `pyproject.toml` and `host.json`
